items.title: Privilege Escalation via File Upload Vulnerability
items.summary: A critical file upload vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to privilege escalation. This issue was addressed in the APSB26-73 security update.

items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution via Improper Encoding/Escaping
items.summary: An improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source could lead to arbitrary code execution. This critical flaw was resolved in the APSB26-73 security update.

items.title: Security Update Available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a security update resolving critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. Successful exploitation could lead to arbitrary code execution, security feature bypass, and privilege escalation.

items.title: Security Feature Bypass via Incorrect Authorization
items.summary: An Incorrect Authorization vulnerability in Adobe Commerce could allow an attacker to bypass security measures and gain unauthorized read and write access without user interaction. This issue is rated with high severity.

items.title: Cross-site Scripting (XSS) in Form Field
items.summary: A vulnerability was found in Adobe Commerce, identified as CVE-2026-47999, related to cross-site scripting in a form field. Further details on severity and impact are typically found in the associated Adobe Security Bulletin.

items.title: Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution in Mirasvit Cache Warmer (PolyShell variant)
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability (CVSS 9.8) in Mirasvit Cache Warmer for Magento and Adobe Commerce. This flaw allows remote code execution via a crafted cookie on any storefront request.

items.title: Authentication Bypass Vulnerability
items.summary: An Incorrect Authorization vulnerability (CVSS 7.5) in Adobe Commerce allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to bypass security checks and gain unauthorized write access. This vulnerability requires no user interaction for exploitation.

items.title: Security Update Available for Adobe Commerce
items.summary: Adobe released a security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, addressing critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities. This bulletin includes fixes for issues like arbitrary code execution, privilege escalation, and security feature bypass.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento REST API
items.summary: Sansec discovered PolyShell, a critical flaw in Magento's REST API that enables unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files by disguising code as an image. Adobe released a fix in Magento 2.4.9, but older versions remain vulnerable.

items.title: Security Update Available for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a security update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, resolving critical, important, and moderate vulnerabilities. Successful exploitation could lead to security feature bypass, denial-of-service, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read.

items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution Vulnerability
items.summary: This critical vulnerability, addressed in APSB26-05, allows for arbitrary code execution in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. It was reported by Akash Hamal.

items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution Vulnerability
items.summary: Part of the APSB26-05 bulletin, this critical vulnerability enables arbitrary code execution in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. It was reported by Akash Hamal.

items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution Vulnerability
items.summary: A critical arbitrary code execution vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source was patched in APSB26-05. This issue was reported by Akash Hamal.

items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution Vulnerability
items.summary: This critical vulnerability, addressed in APSB26-05, allows for arbitrary code execution in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source. It was reported by Akash Hamal.

items.title: Arbitrary Code Execution Vulnerability
items.summary: A critical arbitrary code execution vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source was patched in APSB26-05. This issue was reported by Akash Hamal.
